channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: Integrated Serum Pharmacochemistry, Metabolomics, and Network Pharmacology Uncover Potential Active Components and Mechanisms of Shenling Baizhu Powder in Treating Ulcerative Colitis With Spleen Deficiency and Dampness Stagnation.
item.description: Biomedical chromatography : BMC - This study combined serum pharmacochemistry, metabolomics, and network pharmacology to uncover potential active components and mechanisms of Shenling Baizhu Powder (SLBZP) in treating ulcerative co...

item.title: Sub-inhibitory concentrations of metronidazole alter the proteomic profile and biological effects of extracellular vesicles from Clostridioides difficile.
item.description: BMC microbiology - Colonization with C. difficile is common in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The use of antibiotics can trigger the transition from colonization to active infection, complicating IBD...

item.title: Self-adaptive nanozyme-loaded yeast microcapsules for multifunctional and precise therapy of colitis via intestinal microenvironment remodeling.
item.description: Journal of nanobiotechnology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management remains a significant challenge due to its complex pathogenesis. While current therapies for IBD are effective in symptom management, a radical cure rema...

item.title: Multidimensional dissection of shared genetic susceptibility in ulcerative colitis and colorectal cancer: novel insights from integrative single-cell and multi-omics analysis.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Ulcerative colitis (UC) patients carry a 2.5-fold increased risk of colorectal cancer (CRC), yet the shared multi-scale genetic architecture remains poorly understood. We constructed an integrative...

item.title: Strength of Association: How Useful Is Handgrip Strength as a Functional Biomarker in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease?
The real diagnostic value of serum iron in iron deficiency anemia among patients with inflammatory bowel disease: A retrospective study.
item.description: The Journal of international medical research - ObjectiveThe diagnostic utility of serum iron in identifying iron deficiency anemia among patients with inflammatory bowel disease remains underreported. This study aimed to evaluate the true diagn...

item.title: DUSP family phosphatases in cell signaling, inflammation, and chronic diseases.
item.description: Journal of biomedical science - Review - Multiple members (DUSP1-29) of dual-specificity phosphatase (DSP) family are key regulators of mitogen-activated protein kinases (MAPKs), which regulate numerous physiological responses. Eight DUSP...

item.title: Spatiotemporal analysis reveals distinct inflammatory programs underlying chronic colitis.
item.description: Immunity - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a complex disorder that is often resistant to immunomodulatory treatments. Here, to understand how immune, epithelial, and stromal compartments are rewired durin...

item.title: Predicting trajectories of illness using RNA velocity of whole blood.
item.description: Nature communications - Observational study - Transcriptomic analyses reveal the status of cells, tissues, or organisms, across states of health and disease. RNA velocity adds a temporal dimension to single cell analyses, predicting future tra...

item.title: Aimed at Subtype Discrimination but Yielding a Shared Marker: Integrative Analysis of Blood Transcriptomes Reveals Upregulated TLR5 as a Potential Biomarker for IBD.
item.description: Clinical and experimental gastroenterology - Despite the similarities, Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), the two major subtypes of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), exhibit distinctions. The increasing burden of IBD necessitat...

item.title: Impact of Neutrophils on the Tissue Microenvironment During Intestinal Inflammation.
item.description: Journal of inflammation research - Review - Neutrophils (polymorphonuclear leukocytes, PMN) are abundant innate immune cells that rapidly accumulate at mucosal surfaces during inflammation. While their antimicrobial functions are essential f...

item.title: Faecal calprotectin in perianal fistula: Can a negative test miss isolated Crohn's disease?
item.description: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland - Letter -

item.title: Connecting polygenic disease risk to cell states and regulatory programs through single-cell chromatin accessibility.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Single-cell ATAC-sequencing enables high-resolution mapping of cell states and their putative regulatory elements. The enrichment of genome-wide association study (GWAS) signals within context-spec...

item.title: Dynamin-Related Protein 1 (Drp1) in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Molecular Pathways Connecting Mitochondrial Dynamics with Intestinal Inflammation and Homeostasis.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is characterized by chronic intestinal inflammation, epithelial barrier disruption and immune dysfunction. Alleviating and curing these pathological manifestations ...

item.title: Integrated Multi-Omics Analysis Reveals Activation of the PPAR Signaling Pathway by Koumiss in Experimental Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by persistent mucosal inflammation and dysregulated immune-metabolic responses. Koumiss, a traditional fermented mare's...

item.title: Integrated transcriptome and single-cell RNA sequencing identifies small GTPase-associated biomarkers in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic, multifactorial inflammatory bowel disease. The involvement of small GTPase-related genes (SGRGs) in UC remains poorly defined. This study aims to identify SGRG...
